highlights.title: Meta reveals four Broadcom-built custom AI chips, claims some outperform commercial silicon
India Prepares Smartphone Export Incentives in a Boost for Apple
highlights.description: India is drafting a new round of smartphone manufacturing incentives that would link government subsidies to exports and deeper use of locally made components, in a move that will benefit Apple Inc., Samsung Electronics Co. and their suppliers, according to people familiar with the matter.

highlights.title: Microsoft Pushes for Africa AI Adoption in Challenge to DeepSeek
highlights.description: Microsoft Corp. is making a push for more Africans to adopt its artificial-intelligence tools as the US tech giant competes with China’s DeepSeek for customers from the world’s youngest and fastest-growing population.

highlights.title: AI Coding Startup Cursor in Talks for About $50 Billion Valuation
highlights.description: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in talks with investors for a funding round that would value the startup at about $50 billion, nearly double the valuation it secured last fall, according to people familiar with the efforts.

highlights.title: Apple confirms today’s iOS and iPadOS updates for older devices address the Coruna exploit
highlights.description:
Apple has detailed the security content for iOS 16.7.15, iOS 15.8.7, iPadOS 16.7.15, and iPadOS 15.8.7, confirming that the updates address the Coruna vulnerability disclosed last week by Google and iVerify. Here are the details.
more…

highlights.title: Google sells partial stake in fiber business, becomes minority owner of new venture
highlights.description: Google said its GFiber unit is combing with Astound Broadband and forming a independent fiber provider.

highlights.title: Satellite Firm Iceye Targets €1 Billion Revenue Next Year
highlights.description: Finnish satellite intelligence company Iceye Oy is on track to grow revenue to more than €1 billion ($1.2 billion) next year as a raft of government orders allows it to ramp up production.

highlights.title: Bridge Data Plans Up to $3.9 Billion AI Investment in Singapore
highlights.description: Bridge Data Centres plans to invest as much as S$5 billion ($3.9 billion) in artificial intelligence technology development in its home market of Singapore, seeking to accelerate its growth across Asia.

highlights.title: Google Play will let you try a game before you buy it
highlights.description:
Google Play has introduced a new feature called Game Trials, which will let you play a portion of paid games for free before you commit to buying them. It’s now rolling out to select paid games on mobile, and it’s coming soon to Google Play Games on PC. Titles that offer Game Trials will show a button marked “Try” on their profile pages. When you click it, you’ll see how long you can play the game before you have to buy it. In Google’s example, the survival and horror game Dredge will give you 60 minutes of free play time, after which you’ll get the option to either buy the game or delete it from your device. 
Google has also announced that it’s releasing more paid indie games over the coming months, including Moonlight Peaks, Sledding Game and Low-Budget Repairs. It has launched a new section in the Play store, as well, to feature games optimized for Windows PCs. You can wishlist the games from that section to get a notification when they’re on sale. 
Finally, the company is rolling out Play Games Sidekick, the Gemini-powered Android overlay it announced last year, to select games downloaded from Play. Sidekick can show you relevant info and tools for whatever game you're playing without having to do a search query. But if you’d rather ask other people for gaming advice instead of an AI, you can also look at a game’s Community Posts, a feature now available in English for select titles on their Play pages. 
This article originally appeared on Engadget at https://www.engadget.com/gaming/google-play-will-let-you-try-a-game-before-you-buy-it-051854016.html?src=rss

highlights.title: Indonesia's GoTo Says Keeping Close Eye on Oil Prices
highlights.description: Indonesia's GoTo Group expects full-year earnings to exceed forecasts, driven by strong business momentum. However, CEO Hans Patuwo says the company is keeping a close eye on oil prices and their impact on demand amid the war in the Middle East. In an exclusive interview, he also says GoTo may consider oil surcharges for certain users. (Source: Bloomberg)

business.title: AI Coding Startup Cursor in Talks for About $50 Billion Valuation
business.description: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in talks with investors for a funding round that would value the startup at about $50 billion, nearly double the valuation it secured last fall, according to people familiar with the efforts.

business.title: Asia’s biggest bitcoin buyer now wants to build the BTC ecosystem
business.description: Tokyo-listed Metaplanet is expanding beyond holding BTC into funding, incubating, and granting to companies building bitcoin financial infrastructure.

crypto.title: Crypto code commits fall 75% as developers move to AI projects
crypto.description: Developers are shifting toward artificial intelligence infrastructure as blockchain ecosystems lose contributors across major networks, from Ethereum to Solana.
